University.title: University
University.description: Represents a university with its core information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the University entity.

about.description: A description providing information about the university.

contactEmail.description: The contact email address for the university.

contactPhone.description: The contact phone number for the university.

address.description: The physical address of the university.

socialLinks.description: An array of social media links associated with the university.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents a course or department offered by the university.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

name.description: The name of the course or department.

description.description: A description providing details about the course.

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student enrolled in the university.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

fullName.description: The full name of the student.

Announcement.title: Announcement
Announcement.description: Represents an announcement or notice for students.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Announcement entity.

content.description: The content or message of the announcement.

timestamp.description: The date and time the announcement was created.

Feedback.title: Feedback
Feedback.description: Represents feedback provided by a student.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Feedback entity.

name.description: The name of the person providing feedback.

email.description: The email address of the person providing feedback.

rating.description: A rating given in the feedback (e.g., stars or a numerical value).

Statistic.title: Statistic
Statistic.description: Represents a statistic about the university (e.g., number of students, faculty, etc.).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Statistic entity.

label.description: The label or name of the statistic (e.g., 'Students', 'Faculty').

value.description: The numerical value of the statistic.

definition.description: Stores university data. Each document represents a university.

params.name: universityId
params.description: The unique ID of the university.

params.description: The unique ID of the university.

params.description: The unique ID of the statistic.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to store university-related data, including universities, courses, students, announcements, feedback, and statistics. The structure prioritizes path-based ownership and avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies to ensure data integrity and security. The main principle applied here is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization**.

- **Universities:** Stored at the root level in the `universities` collection. This allows easy listing and management of universities.
- **Courses:** Stored as subcollections under each university (`/universities/{universityId}/courses/{courseId}`). This reflects the one-to-many relationship between universities and courses.
- **Students:** Stored as subcollections under each university (`/universities/{universityId}/students/{studentId}`). This represents the one-to-many relationship between universities and students. Additionally, `courseId` is included to enable efficient querying of students within a specific course.
- **Announcements:** Stored as subcollections under each university (`/universities/{universityId}/announcements/{announcementId}`). This allows for university-specific announcements.
- **Feedback:** Stored as subcollections under each university (`/universities/{universityId}/feedback/{feedbackId}`).  This structure ensures that feedback is associated with the correct university.
- **Statistics:** Stored as subcollections under each university (`/universities/{universityId}/statistics/{statisticId}`). This allows for easy management and retrieval of university-specific statistics.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** The structure avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies (`get()`). Each entity contains the `universityId` making it possible to validate security rules without needing to fetch parent data. For example, in the `/universities/{universityId}/courses/{courseId}` collection, checking that the `universityId` matches the `{universityId}` parameter ensures that the user has access to the parent university. This also applies to students, announcements, feedback, and statistics.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The structure enables secure `list` operations. By segregating data based on university ID, it's possible to create security rules that only allow listing of resources associated with a specific university, preventing unauthorized access to data from other universities.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):** The data is segregated based on entity type (universities, courses, students, etc.) and each university has its own subcollections. This ensures that all documents within a collection or subcollection share the same security requirements, simplifying the security rules.
